{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成}} [[Category:Shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成}} [[Category:Shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成}} [[Category:Shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成}} [[Category:Shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''

It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | Alternate names = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related (Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}}
