{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | alternate_name = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | alternate_name = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} | Sacred Object = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}} statue | type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = (Legendary) {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | alternate_name = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yoshioka, Gunma|ja|吉岡町}}, {{ill|Kitagunma District|ja|北群馬郡}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}) and ranked as the third shrine ({{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}) of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}} * {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ}} The {{ill|Shintai|ja|神体}} is a single-wood-carved statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}, estimated to be from the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''{{ill|Shintōshū|ja|神道集}}'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} ({{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}})}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} (Yuzen Shrine) in {{ill|Shibukawa, Gunma|ja|渋川市}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 三宮神社 |画像 = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[北群馬郡]][[吉岡町]]大字大久保字宮1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=三宮神社}} |祭神 = [[ホオリ|彦火火出見命]]
[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]
[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] |神体 = [[十一面観音]]像 |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
[[上野国]][[一宮|三宮]]
旧[[村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]） |本殿 =一間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''三宮神社'''
（里宮）|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Gunma|label=[[伊香保神社]]
（山宮）|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''三宮神社'''（さんのみやじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[北群馬郡]][[吉岡町]]大久保にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、[[上野国]][[一宮|三宮]]。旧社格は[[村社]]{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}}。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱{{Sfn|三宮神社（平凡社）|1987}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}}。 * [[ホオリ|彦火火出見命]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] * [[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]] [[神体]]は一木造の[[十一面観音]]像（[[室町時代]]作と推定、像高90センチメートル）である。『[[神道集]]』には伊香保神里宮の[[本地仏]]は十一面と見え、その記載と一致する。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[伊香保神社]]（[[渋川市]]伊香保町伊香保）}}{{small|[[伊香保温泉]]街の一角。三宮神社はその里宮とされる。}}]] 創建は[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]）の[[勧請]]と伝えるが、詳らかでない{{Sfn|三宮神社（平凡社）|1987}}。 [[中世]]の『[[神道集]]』では、「伊香保大明神」について「女体ハ里ヘ下セ給テ三宮渋河保ニ立セ御在ス、本地ハ十一面也」と見えることから、当社は[[伊香保神社]]（湯前神、[[渋川市]]伊香保町伊香保）の里宮にあたると考えられている{{Sfn|三宮神社（平凡社）|1987}}。「伊香保」とは噴火の激しい[[榛名山]]を「厳つ峰（いかつほ）」と称したことによるとされるが{{Sfn|伊加保神社（式内社）|1986}}、山宮の鎮座地は噴火に伴う堆積層のため耕作には不向きで、[[6世紀]]中葉頃の最後の噴火後数百年を経て湧出した温泉で発展した地になる{{Sfn|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}。そのため、温泉湧出以前は里宮の三宮神社が祭祀中心地であったと見られ、その様子は現在も社名に「三宮」、すなわち上野国三宮を掲げることからも示唆される。上野国では以上の類例として、[[二宮赤城神社]]（里宮、[[前橋市]]二之宮町）と[[赤城神社 (前橋市三夜沢町)|三夜沢赤城神社]]（山宮、前橋市三夜沢町）の関係がある。 当社の鎮座地は『[[和名抄]]』に見える上野国[[群馬郡]]有馬郷に比定されることから、奉斎氏族は古代氏族の有馬氏であったとされる。『[[新撰姓氏録]]』右京皇別 垂水公条では[[豊城入彦命]]（[[上毛野氏]]祖）子孫として「阿利真公」の人物名が見え、上毛野氏の一族と推測されている{{Sfn|伊加保神社（式内社）|1986}}。当地周辺では、一族のものと推測される多くの古墳も見られる{{Sfn|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}。 なお、「三宮」の社名は祭神が3柱であるためとも、彦火火出見命が[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵命]]の第三皇子であるためともする異伝があるが詳細は明らかでない{{Sfn|三宮神社（平凡社）|1987}}。 === 概史 === [[六国史|国史]]では、「伊賀保社」は[[承和 (日本)|承和]]2年（[[835年]]）に名神に列したと見えるほか、「伊賀保神」の[[神階]]が承和6年（[[839年]]）に従五位下、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）に正五位下、貞観11年（[[869年]]）に正五位上、貞観18年（[[876年]]）に従四位下、[[元慶]]4年（[[880年]]）に従四位上に昇叙された旨の記載が見える{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000|pp=279-280}}。その神階は上野国において、[[一之宮貫前神社|貫前神]]に準じ、[[赤城神社|赤城神]]と同格になる。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では、[[上野国]][[群馬郡]]に「伊加保神社 名神大」として、貫前神社・赤城神社とともに[[名神大社]]として記載されている{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000|pp=279-280}}。 [[長元]]3年（[[1030年]]）頃の『上野国交替実録帳』では、「正一位伊賀保明神社」の項に玉殿1宇・幣殿1宇・鳥居2基・向屋1宇・美豆垣1廻・荒垣1廻・舞人陪従屋1宇・厨屋1宇と記載がある{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000|pp=279-280}}。しかしこの社殿規模は貫前社・赤城社に劣り、他の式内小社・式外社と同格になる{{Sfn|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}。そのためこの頃までには衰微したものと考えられ、伊香保の地に移ったのもこの頃と推測される{{Sfn|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}。 『上野国神名帳』では、いずれも鎮守十社のうちで、総社本では3番目に「正一位伊賀保大明神」、一宮本では2番目に「正一位伊賀保大明神」、群書類従本では3番目に「正一位伊香保大明神」と記されている。同帳では、関連神名として「[[若伊香保神社|若伊賀保神]]」「伊賀保若御子明神」「伊賀保木戸明神」の記載も見える{{Sfn|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}。 [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]成立の『[[神道集]]』では、「上野国九ヶ所大明神事」や「上野国第三宮伊香保大明神事」に記述が見える{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000|pp=279-280}}。これらによると、伊香保神は貫前神（[[一宮]]）・赤城神（二宮）に次ぐ[[上野国]]三宮であるほか、湯前にある男体（[[本地仏]]：[[薬師如来]]）と、渋川保三宮の里宮にある女体（本地仏：十一面観音）とから成るという{{Sfn|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}。 [[近世]]以降の三宮神社は衰微し、詳細は明らかでない。対して山宮は[[伊香保温泉]]とともに発展し、近世まで「湯前大明神」と称していたが、[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に「伊香保神社」と改称している{{Sfn|伊加保神社（式内社）|1986}}。現在では、里宮・山宮の間に祭祀関係はない。 === 神階 ===
上野国主要3神の神階　　　　
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:100%;background-color:#ffffff;white-space: nowrap" !年!![[一之宮貫前神社|貫前神]]
（抜鋒神）!![[赤城神社|赤城神]]!!伊賀保神 |- |[[835年]]|| || ||名神 |- |[[839年]]||無位
→従五位下||無位
→従五位下||無位

* [[六国史]]時代における[[神階]]奉叙の記録 ** [[承和 (日本)|承和]]2年（[[835年]]）9月29日、名神に列す （『[[続日本後紀]]』） - 表記は「伊賀保社」。 ** 承和6年（[[839年]]）6月、無位から従五位下 （『続日本後紀』） - 表記は「伊賀保神」。 ** [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）6月20日、従五位上から正五位下 （『[[日本三代実録]]』） - 表記は「伊賀保神」。 ** 貞観11年（[[869年]]）12月25日、正五位下から正五位上 （『日本三代実録』） - 表記は「伊賀保神」。 ** 貞観18年（[[876年]]）4月10日、正五位上から従四位下 （『日本三代実録』） - 表記は「伊賀保神」。 ** [[元慶]]4年（[[880年]]）5月25日、従四位下から従四位上 （『日本三代実録』） - 表記は「伊賀保神」。 * 六国史以後 ** 正一位 （『上野国神名帳』） 表記は「伊賀保大明神」または「伊香保大明神」。 == 境内 == File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) honden.JPG|本殿（吉岡町指定文化財） File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) kaguraden.JPG|神楽殿 File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) entrance.JPG|境内入り口 == 文化財 == === 吉岡町指定文化財 === * 重要文化財（有形文化財） ** 三宮神社（御神体を含む） - 1988年（昭和63年）2月22日指定{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}}。本殿は一間社流造銅板葺で[[江戸時代]]の[[嘉永]]3年（[[1850年]]）に改築を受けており19世紀前期の建築と推定される。 * 重要無形文化財 ** 溝祭三宮神社獅子舞 - 2003年（平成15年）5月22日指定{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}}。 ** 三宮神社太々神楽三楽講 - 2011年（平成23年）11月29日指定{{Cite web |title=三楽講 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E6%A5%BD%E8%AC%9B |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |editor=[[谷川健一]]編 |author=田島桂男 |year=1984 |chapter=伊香保神社 |title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 11 関東 |publisher=[[白水社]] |isbn=4560025118 |ref={{Harvid|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=坂井久能|year=1986|chapter=伊加保神社|title=式内社調査報告 第13巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|伊加保神社（式内社）|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=三宮神社|title=日本歴史地名大系 10 群馬県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|三宮神社（平凡社）|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究|publisher=岩田書院|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[伊香保神社]] * [[若伊香保神社]] * [[南下古墳群]] * [[三津屋古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html 伊加保神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」{{リンク切れ|date=2024年10月}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さんのみやしんしや}} [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:吉岡町の建築物]] [[Category:吉岡町の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社|名さんのみやしんしや]] [[Category:上野国|社3]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 三宮神社 |画像 = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[北群馬郡]][[吉岡町]]大字大久保字宮1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=三宮神社}} |祭神 = [[ホオリ|彦火火出見命]]
[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]
[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] |神体 = [[十一面観音]]像 |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
[[上野国]][[一宮|三宮]]
旧[[村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]） |本殿 =一間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''三宮神社'''
（里宮）|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Gunma|label=[[伊香保神社]]
（山宮）|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''三宮神社'''（さんのみやじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[北群馬郡]][[吉岡町]]大久保にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、[[上野国]][[一宮|三宮]]。旧社格は[[村社]]{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}}。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱{{Sfn|三宮神社（平凡社）|1987}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}}。 * [[ホオリ|彦火火出見命]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] * [[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]] [[神体]]は一木造の[[十一面観音]]像（[[室町時代]]作と推定、像高90センチメートル）である。『[[神道集]]』には伊香保神里宮の[[本地仏]]は十一面と見え、その記載と一致する。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[伊香保神社]]（[[渋川市]]伊香保町伊香保）}}{{small|[[伊香保温泉]]街の一角。三宮神社はその里宮とされる。}}]] 創建は[[天平勝宝]]2年（[[750年]]）の[[勧請]]と伝えるが、詳らかでない{{Sfn|三宮神社（平凡社）|1987}}。 [[中世]]の『[[神道集]]』では、「伊香保大明神」について「女体ハ里ヘ下セ給テ三宮渋河保ニ立セ御在ス、本地ハ十一面也」と見えることから、当社は[[伊香保神社]]（湯前神、[[渋川市]]伊香保町伊香保）の里宮にあたると考えられている{{Sfn|三宮神社（平凡社）|1987}}。「伊香保」とは噴火の激しい[[榛名山]]を「厳つ峰（いかつほ）」と称したことによるとされるが{{Sfn|伊加保神社（式内社）|1986}}、山宮の鎮座地は噴火に伴う堆積層のため耕作には不向きで、[[6世紀]]中葉頃の最後の噴火後数百年を経て湧出した温泉で発展した地になる{{Sfn|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}。そのため、温泉湧出以前は里宮の三宮神社が祭祀中心地であったと見られ、その様子は現在も社名に「三宮」、すなわち上野国三宮を掲げることからも示唆される。上野国では以上の類例として、[[二宮赤城神社]]（里宮、[[前橋市]]二之宮町）と[[赤城神社 (前橋市三夜沢町)|三夜沢赤城神社]]（山宮、前橋市三夜沢町）の関係がある。 当社の鎮座地は『[[和名抄]]』に見える上野国[[群馬郡]]有馬郷に比定されることから、奉斎氏族は古代氏族の有馬氏であったとされる。『[[新撰姓氏録]]』右京皇別 垂水公条では[[豊城入彦命]]（[[上毛野氏]]祖）子孫として「阿利真公」の人物名が見え、上毛野氏の一族と推測されている{{Sfn|伊加保神社（式内社）|1986}}。当地周辺では、一族のものと推測される多くの古墳も見られる{{Sfn|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}。 なお、「三宮」の社名は祭神が3柱であるためとも、彦火火出見命が[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵命]]の第三皇子であるためともする異伝があるが詳細は明らかでない{{Sfn|三宮神社（平凡社）|1987}}。 === 概史 === [[六国史|国史]]では、「伊賀保社」は[[承和 (日本)|承和]]2年（[[835年]]）に名神に列したと見えるほか、「伊賀保神」の[[神階]]が承和6年（[[839年]]）に従五位下、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）に正五位下、貞観11年（[[869年]]）に正五位上、貞観18年（[[876年]]）に従四位下、[[元慶]]4年（[[880年]]）に従四位上に昇叙された旨の記載が見える{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000|pp=279-280}}。その神階は上野国において、[[一之宮貫前神社|貫前神]]に準じ、[[赤城神社|赤城神]]と同格になる。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では、[[上野国]][[群馬郡]]に「伊加保神社 名神大」として、貫前神社・赤城神社とともに[[名神大社]]として記載されている{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000|pp=279-280}}。 [[長元]]3年（[[1030年]]）頃の『上野国交替実録帳』では、「正一位伊賀保明神社」の項に玉殿1宇・幣殿1宇・鳥居2基・向屋1宇・美豆垣1廻・荒垣1廻・舞人陪従屋1宇・厨屋1宇と記載がある{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000|pp=279-280}}。しかしこの社殿規模は貫前社・赤城社に劣り、他の式内小社・式外社と同格になる{{Sfn|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}。そのためこの頃までには衰微したものと考えられ、伊香保の地に移ったのもこの頃と推測される{{Sfn|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}。 『上野国神名帳』では、いずれも鎮守十社のうちで、総社本では3番目に「正一位伊賀保大明神」、一宮本では2番目に「正一位伊賀保大明神」、群書類従本では3番目に「正一位伊香保大明神」と記されている。同帳では、関連神名として「[[若伊香保神社|若伊賀保神]]」「伊賀保若御子明神」「伊賀保木戸明神」の記載も見える{{Sfn|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}。 [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]成立の『[[神道集]]』では、「上野国九ヶ所大明神事」や「上野国第三宮伊香保大明神事」に記述が見える{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000|pp=279-280}}。これらによると、伊香保神は貫前神（[[一宮]]）・赤城神（二宮）に次ぐ[[上野国]]三宮であるほか、湯前にある男体（[[本地仏]]：[[薬師如来]]）と、渋川保三宮の里宮にある女体（本地仏：十一面観音）とから成るという{{Sfn|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}。 [[近世]]以降の三宮神社は衰微し、詳細は明らかでない。対して山宮は[[伊香保温泉]]とともに発展し、近世まで「湯前大明神」と称していたが、[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に「伊香保神社」と改称している{{Sfn|伊加保神社（式内社）|1986}}。現在では、里宮・山宮の間に祭祀関係はない。 === 神階 ===
上野国主要3神の神階

（抜鋒神）!![[赤城神社|赤城神]]!!伊賀保神 |- |[[835年]]|| || ||名神 |- |[[839年]]||無位

→正三位勲七等|| || |}

1988年（昭和63年）2月22日指定{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}}。本殿は一間社流造銅板葺で[[江戸時代]]の[[嘉永]]3年（[[1850年]]）に改築を受けており19世紀前期の建築と推定される。 * 重要無形文化財 ** 溝祭三宮神社獅子舞 - 2003年（平成15年）5月22日指定{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}}。 ** 三宮神社太々神楽三楽講 - 2011年（平成23年）11月29日指定{{Cite web |title=三楽講 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E6%A5%BD%E8%AC%9B |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |editor=[[谷川健一]]編 |author=田島桂男 |year=1984 |chapter=伊香保神社 |title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 11 関東 |publisher=[[白水社]] |isbn=4560025118 |ref={{Harvid|伊香保神社（神々）|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=坂井久能|year=1986|chapter=伊加保神社|title=式内社調査報告 第13巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|伊加保神社（式内社）|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=三宮神社|title=日本歴史地名大系 10 群馬県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|三宮神社（平凡社）|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究|publisher=岩田書院|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[伊香保神社]] * [[若伊香保神社]] * [[南下古墳群]] * [[三津屋古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html 伊加保神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」{{リンク切れ|date=2024年10月}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さんのみやしんしや}} [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:吉岡町の建築物]] [[Category:吉岡町の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社|名さんのみやしんしや]] [[Category:上野国|社3]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sannomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], Ōkubo Miyamae 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|26|22.1|N|139|0|49.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannomiya Shrine}} | Deities = [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]]
[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]]
[[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] | Sacred Object = [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]] statue | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}})
[[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] [[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | Established = (Legendary) [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | alternate_name = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏|1=Honji_suijaku#Honji Buddha|lt=Honji Buddha|2=ja|3=本地垂迹|4=fr|5=Honji suijaku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honji Suijaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Honji_suijaku#Honji Buddha|qq=}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/21st Century COE Program|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|version=102298901|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sannomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]] [[ja:三宮神社 (吉岡町)]] [[en:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ueno Province]]

(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏|1=Honji_suijaku#Honji Buddha|lt=Honji Buddha|2=ja|3=本地垂迹|4=fr|5=Honji suijaku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honji Suijaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Honji_suijaku#Honji Buddha|qq=}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/21st Century COE Program|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|version=102298901|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sannomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]] [[ja:三宮神社 (吉岡町)]] [[en:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ueno Province]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:吉岡町の建築物]]

In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏|1=Honji_suijaku#Honji Buddha|lt=Honji Buddha|2=ja|3=本地垂迹|4=fr|5=Honji suijaku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honji Suijaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Honji_suijaku#Honji Buddha|qq=}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - (Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the {{ill|Honji Suijaku|ja|本地仏|1=Honji_suijaku#Honji Buddha|lt=Honji Buddha|2=ja|3=本地垂迹|4=fr|5=Honji suijaku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honji Suijaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Honji_suijaku#Honji Buddha|qq=}} of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/21st Century COE Program|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|version=102298901|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sannomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]] [[ja:三宮神社 (吉岡町)]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ueno Province]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:吉岡町の建築物]] [[Category:吉岡町の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] {{Commons category|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - (Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/21st Century COE Program|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|version=102298901|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]] [[ja:三宮神社 (吉岡町)]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ueno Province]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:吉岡町の建築物]] [[Category:吉岡町の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] {{Commons category|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}}

According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/21st Century COE Program|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|version=102298901|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]] [[ja:三宮神社 (吉岡町)]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ueno Province]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:吉岡町の建築物]] [[Category:吉岡の Municipal History]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:群馬県内のMunicipal designated tangible cultural property]] {{Commons category|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}}

(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = (Legendary) [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | alternate_name = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/21st Century COE Program|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|version=102298901|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]] [[ja:三宮神社 (吉岡町)]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ueno Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:吉岡町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yoshioka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}}
