According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/21st Century COE Program|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|version=102298901|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]] [[ja:三宮神社 (吉岡町)]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ueno Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Architecture of Yoshioka Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yoshioka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = (Legendary) [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | alternate_name = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} | location = {{ill|Yoshioka|qid=Q1154580}} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/21st Century COE Program|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|version=102298901|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]] [[ja:三宮神社 (吉岡町)]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ueno Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Architecture of Yoshioka Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yoshioka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/21st Century COE Program|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|version=102298901|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]] [[ja:三宮神社 (吉岡町)]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ueno Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Architecture of Yoshioka Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yoshioka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]][[sn:Shikinaisha]][[sn:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]][[sn:Shikinaisha]] [[en:Sannomiya Shrine (Yoshioka Town)]] {{Commons category|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}}

(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - (Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - (Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} | location = {{ill|Yoshioka|qid=Q1154580}} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - (Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} | location = {{ill|Yoshioka|qid=Q1154580}} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - (Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} | location = {{ill|Yoshioka|WD=Q1154580}} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|qq=|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] | Sacred Object = [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]] statue | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
[[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] [[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = (Legendary) [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE) | Main Hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri | alternate_name = | Sacred Sites = | Festivals = | Rituals = | Map = {{location map+|Japan Gunma|float=center|border=none|position=none|width=200|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label='''Sannomiya Shrine'''
(Satomiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=22.07|lon_deg=139|lon_min=0|lon_sec=49.50|position=right|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Gunma|label={{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}}
(Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} | location = {{ill|Yoshioka|WD=Q1154580}} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/21st Century COE Program|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|version=102298901|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:三宮神社 (吉岡町)]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ueno Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Architecture of Yoshioka Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yoshioka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} {{wikidata link|Q21655265}}

Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - (Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} | location = {{ill|Yoshioka|WD=Q1154580}} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and Ranked as the third shrine ([[:en:Sannomiya|Sannomiya]]) of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=78-80}} == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:{{Cite book|和書 |title=吉岡村誌 |date=1980-11-15 |year=1980 |publisher=吉岡村教育委員会 |pages=925-928 |editor=吉岡村誌編纂室}} * [[:en:Hoori|Hoori]] * [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime]] * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a single-wood-carved statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]], estimated to be from the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] and standing 90 cm tall. According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - Kokugakuin University {{ill|21st Century COE Program|ja|神道・神社史料集成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/21st Century COE Program|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|三宮神社 (吉岡町)|version=102298901|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yoshioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:三宮神社 (吉岡町)]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ueno Province]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Architecture of Yoshioka Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yoshioka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} {{wikidata link|Q21655265}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

According to the ''[[:en:Shintōshū|Shintōshū]]'', the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] of Ikaho Shrine is an Eleven-faced Kannon, matching this description. == History == === Establishment === [[File:伊香保神社 Ikaho shrine - panoramio.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} ([[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]])}}{{small|Located in the {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}} area, Sannomiya Shrine serves as its Satomiya.}} The shrine is traditionally believed to have been established in [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 2 (750 CE), though detailed records are lacking. In medieval times, the ''Shintōshū'' describes the "Ikaho Daimyōjin" as having a female form enshrined at the Satomiya in Sannomiya Shibukawa, with its honji being the Eleven-faced Kannon. This suggests Sannomiya Shrine served as the Satomiya of {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} (Yuzen Shrine) in [[:en:Shibukawa, Gunma|Shibukawa, Gunma]]. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|吉岡町指定有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Town Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' Sannomiya Shrine (including sacred objects) – Designated on February 22, 1988.{{Cite web |title=三宮神社 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E4%B8%89%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} ''' Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property: Sannomiya Shrine Lion Dance – Designated on May 22, 2003.{{Cite web |title=溝祭獅子舞 {{!}} 吉岡町教育委員会 |url=https://www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp/%E6%8C%87%E5%AE%9A%E6%96%87%E5%8C%96%E8%B2%A1/%E6%BA%9D%E7%A5%AD%E7%8D%85%E5%AD%90%E8%88%9E |website=www.net.yoshioka.ed.jp |access-date=2024-10-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220841}} * {{ill|Waka Ikaho Shrine|ja|若伊香保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Ikaho Shrine|1=Wakaikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655266}} == References == == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250301.html Sannomiya Shrine Information] - (Yamamiya)|lat_deg=36|lat_min=29|lat_sec=45.31|lon_deg=138|lon_min=54|lon_sec=57|position=left|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} | location = {{ill|Yoshioka|WD=Q1154580}} }} [[File:Sannomiya-jinja (Yoshioka) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}} '''Sannomiya Shrine''' (三宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yoshioka, Gunma|Yoshioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Kitagunma District, Gunma|Kitagunma District]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]].
